initial_draft:
```
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> The other player is offering X for 30 ZUP. I am willing to pay up to 60 ZUP for X, so 30 ZUP is a good deal. I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of X for 30 ZUP. </message>
```

iterations.feedback:

```
<critique>

2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting a trade for 30 ZUP when my maximum valuation is 60 ZUP is a perfectly reasonable and payoff-maximizing decision.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered a price of 30 ZUP, and I'm accepting it. This is likely to be accepted.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> explains why accepting the trade is a good idea, aligning with the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The <message> also reinforces this acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I am respecting the proposal budget and turn constraints.
</critique>
```
